missing_package_delivered_002: To find a missing package marked as delivered, verify your shipping address, check for delivery notices, look around the delivery location, and ask household members or neighbors. If the package doesn't arrive within 48 hours, contact the carrier, third-party seller, or Amazon Customer Service for further assistance.
push_notifications_tracking_003: Enable push notifications in your device settings and the Amazon app to track shipment updates. Configure your iOS or Android notification preferences to allow time-sensitive alerts for the best delivery tracking experience.
late_delivery_007: If an Amazon delivery is late due to weather, traffic, or high demand, track the package and wait 48 hours past the estimated delivery date. Contact Customer Service or the seller if it doesn't arrive, and claim a delivery fee refund for late guaranteed deliveries.
amz_fee_001: Return shipping fees vary based on item type, shipping method, and handling requirements. Fees may apply when selecting paid shipping, returning heavy or bulky items, or having an unusually high return rate.
amz_fee_002: Customers face late return fees if items are not returned before the required date. You will be charged 20 percent of the item price within the first 30 days and 100 percent after that period.
amz_fee_003: Damage fees apply if returned items are damaged, missing parts, show signs of use, or lack original tags and packaging. Customers may be charged up to 50 percent of the item price, or 100 percent for Amazon Luxury items.
amz_fee_004: A full 100 percent restocking fee applies to certain returned products. This includes opened software, activated video games, opened collectible cards, and board games or figurines missing parts or packaging.
amz_ref_002: Refund processing times depend on the payment method used for the return. Amazon Gift Cards take 2 to 3 hours, credit cards take 3 to 5 business days, and debit cards or checking accounts can take up to 10 business days.
amz_int_004: Heavy and bulky item returns are defined as products weighing 50 pounds or more, or exceeding specific length and girth dimensions. These returns may require specialty carrier pickups, home collection services, and variable return shipping fees.
multiple_addresses_001: The multi-address delivery feature allows you to purchase multiple items in a single order and ship them to different addresses. Simply add items to your cart, proceed to checkout, and select unique delivery locations or gift options for each item.
missing_packing_slip_001: If a packing slip is missing, you can print an order invoice directly from Your Orders in your account. A physical packing slip is not required to make returns for single-box deliveries, reducing packaging waste.
sign_for_order_001: Some orders require an adult signature upon delivery. If unavailable, you can change the shipping address before dispatch, contact the carrier to arrange collection, or refuse the delivery if collection is impossible.
about_security_codes_001: Amazon requires security codes to verify your identity and protect sensitive account information. If you do not receive a code via SMS, verify your mobile number, check your device settings to unblock premium text messages, and request a new code.
approve_a_teens_order_001: Parents can review and approve existing Teen account orders via email, text message, or the Your Orders page. Orders under a preset spending limit do not require approval, and unapproved requests expire after 48 hours.
change_your_subscription_email_preferenc: Customize your subscription email preferences for Prime memberships, Subscribe and Save items, and marketing communications through the Communications Preference Center. Updates to your email settings may take 1 to 5 business days to process.
change_your_account_settings_001: You can update your Amazon account information, such as your name, phone number, email address, and password, through the Login and Security page. Simply select edit next to the desired field, follow the prompts, and save your changes.
change_your_language_preference_001: Customize your preferred language on Amazon by accessing the Language Settings or clicking the country flag icon. Changes made on desktop do not sync to the mobile app, and some communications may still appear in the default language.
change_your_password_001: Update your Amazon password by accessing the Login and Security section in Your Account. Choose to edit your password, follow the provided instructions, and save your changes to secure your account.
manage_a_multiple_accounts_use_the_same_: Amazon prohibits using the same email address for multiple accounts. To resolve a duplicate email error, sign in to your default account or change the email address associated with your secondary account.
manage_an_email_address_already_in_use_e: If you encounter an email address already in use error, try signing in, resetting your password, or using an alternate email alias or phone number. To reuse an email on a new account, you must first release it by changing the address on your existing account.
recover_your_account_after_twostep_verif: If Two-Step Verification fails, recover your account by verifying your identity with a government-issued ID. Submit a photo of your ID with sensitive numbers concealed, and wait 1 to 2 days for verification to regain access.
removing_a_teen_login_from_an_amazon_hou: Teens aged 18 or older can transition from a Teen Login to a regular Amazon account to gain independent shopping privileges. The adult or teen can manually remove the profile from the Amazon Household, carrying over order history, wishlists, and gift card balances.
reset_your_password_001: Reset a forgotten Amazon password by navigating to the password assistance page and entering your associated email or phone number. Verify your identity with a One Time Password to securely create a new active password.
set_order_approvals_for_a_teen_login_001: Parents can configure order approval settings and spending limits for existing Teen accounts via Manage Your Household. You can choose to skip manual approvals while still receiving itemized notifications and retaining cancellation rights.
unsubscribe_from_amazon_marketing_001: Stop receiving promotional emails and direct mail by updating your settings in the Communications Preference Center. Uncheck marketing email options and save your changes to opt out of product recommendations and deal alerts.
what_is_twostep_verification_001: Two-Step Verification enhances account security by requiring a unique code via text or authenticator app alongside your password during login. Enable or disable this feature through the Login and Security section of Your Account.
about_currency_of_preference_on_internat: This document lists numerous global currencies, including the Australian Dollar, Euro, Japanese Yen, and British Pound, available for international shopping. It serves as a reference for supported currencies on the platform.
amazon_currency_converter_supported_curr: The Amazon Currency Converter supports multiple global currencies for use with eligible American Express, MasterCard, and Visa payment methods. The table details supported card types and estimated shipping windows of 14 or 30 days for each currency.
select_your_card_currency_for_amazon_cur: Use the Amazon Currency Converter to pay in your local card currency by updating your payment method during checkout. Ensure your card currency is accurately selected on desktop or mobile to view the total order cost in your preferred currency before purchasing.
resolve_a_declined_payment_001: If a payment is declined, verify your credit limit, billing information, and bank security policies. Retry the transaction by accessing Your Orders and either updating the current payment method or submitting a new card.
unknown_amazon_payment_charges_001: Identify unknown Amazon charges by comparing bank statement descriptors to common services like Amazon Prime, Amazon Fresh, or digital purchases. This reference table helps distinguish between Amazon marketplace payments, digital subscriptions, and standard retail orders.
identifying_a_scam_001: Protect yourself from impersonation scams by recognizing red flags such as false urgency, requests for personal information, off-site payments, or gift card demands. Report suspicious emails, calls, or unexpected order notifications directly to Amazon to ensure account security.
report_suspicious_activity_001: Report suspicious products, sellers, or scams directly through the Amazon product page or Customer Service portal. Contact local law enforcement and utilize provided forms to report illegal items, stolen goods, or fraudulent communications.
verify_your_email_for_a_new_account_001: Verify ownership of your email address by clicking the link sent to your inbox when creating a new Amazon account. If you do not receive the email, double-check your spelling and spam folder, keeping in mind that existing emails cannot be reused.
add_a_shopping_profile_001: Personalize your Amazon experience by adding up to five additional adult shopping profiles to your account. Navigate to Manage Profiles to create, edit, or remove profiles, ensuring personalized recommendations for each user.
add_delete_and_manage_addresses_001: Manage your delivery preferences by adding, editing, or deleting shipping locations in the Your Addresses section. Set a default address for faster checkout, but note that modifying saved addresses will not update pending orders or wish lists.
archived_orders_001: View previously archived purchases by searching or applying date filters in the Your Orders section. To maintain separate order histories for family members, use Amazon Family to link accounts while sharing Prime benefits.
bereavement_support_001: The Amazon bereavement support team assists authorized estate managers in closing accounts, terminating subscriptions, or stopping deliveries for deceased customers. Verification requires a death certificate, proof of legal authorization, photo ID, and the account's contact information.
cancel_items_and_orders_001: Cancel physical items or unshipped orders by accessing View or edit order in the Your Orders section. Orders shipped by third-party sellers may require seller approval, while shipped Amazon orders must be refused at delivery or returned.
change_your_order_information_001: Modify un-dispatched orders by updating the delivery address, billing details, or payment methods in the Orders section of Your Account. For items from third-party sellers or Amazon Fresh, you must use specific contact or modification tools to adjust your delivery.
change_your_default_payment_methods_001: Set or update your default payment method by navigating to Your Wallet and editing your preferred card. Save your changes and authenticate the request to streamline future purchases and subscriptions.
check_your_gift_card_balance_001: Check your stored Amazon Gift Card balance and review transaction history through the Gift Cards section of Your Account. Physical gift cards without printed values can be verified by cross-referencing the serial number with order details.
check_your_credit_and_benefit_balances_0: Monitor your available promotions, credits, and benefit balances by accessing Your Account. Keep in mind that credit activity data is only accessible for a rolling 365-day period.
manage_payment_methods_001: Add, edit, or remove credit and debit cards, including Tap to Pay options, through Your Payments in Your Account. Flexible Spending or Health Savings Accounts can be registered as credit cards, but you are responsible for purchasing eligible products.
manage_thirdparty_apps_and_services_with: Control the data shared via Login with Amazon by managing third-party app connections in your account settings. You can revoke access at any time, though providers may still retain previously shared information.
manage_your_amazon_accounts_on_mobile_de: Switch between multiple Amazon accounts on a single mobile device using the Switch Accounts feature in app settings. Avoid using this feature on shared devices, and deregister lost devices remotely via the Manage Your Content and Devices page.
manage_your_backup_payment_methods_001: Enable and configure backup payment methods in Your Payments to prevent delays on eligible orders and subscriptions. You can disable unwanted backup cards, though this may result in fulfillment delays if your primary method fails.
request_the_closure_of_your_account_and_: Submit a permanent account closure and data deletion request to terminate access to all global Amazon services, digital content, and business platforms. Verify the closure via email or text within five days, and repeat the process for any multiple accounts.
sign_out_of_your_amazon_account_in_the_a: To securely sign out of the Amazon shopping app on an iOS or Android device, open the main menu. Scroll to the bottom of the page, select the sign-out option, and confirm your choice.
sign_out_of_your_amazon_account_on_the_a: Sign out of your Amazon account on iOS and Android mobile devices by accessing the hamburger menu in the app. Scroll to the bottom of the screen and confirm your sign-out request.
use_login_with_amazon_001: Login with Amazon allows you to securely access third-party websites and apps using your Amazon credentials without sharing your password. You can review and revoke third-party data access permissions at any time through your account settings.
use_switch_accounts_001: The Switch Accounts feature lets you seamlessly transition between multiple Amazon profiles in a browser without repeatedly signing in. For security purposes, avoid using this feature on shared or public computers to protect your personal information.
what_are_mobile_phone_number_accounts_00: Mobile phone number accounts allow users to create and manage an Amazon profile entirely via a smartphone number instead of an email. To receive order updates and third-party seller communications, you can add an email address in the Login and Security settings.
what_happens_when_i_close_my_account_003: Closing your Amazon account results in the permanent loss of access to order history, Prime memberships, digital content, and Amazon devices. Connected services, including AWS, Seller Central, Audible, and gift card balances, will also be disabled or deleted.
about_us_state_sales_and_use_taxes_002: This document lists the US states and territories, including California, New York, and Puerto Rico, that may be subject to sales and use tax. It serves as a geographic reference for domestic taxation policies.
print_an_invoice_001: Print an official order invoice by navigating to Your Orders once a package has shipped. Select the invoice option and use your browser's print function to generate a physical copy for your records.
vat_rates_001: This table outlines the standard Value Added Tax rates for various European and international countries. Rates range from 8.1 percent in Switzerland to 27 percent in Hungary for standard goods and services.
vat_rates_002: This comprehensive table specifies the Value Added Tax rates for Kindle Books, Kindle Newsstand, and other digital products across multiple countries. It highlights varying tax structures, including reduced or zero-percent rates for specific digital and educational content in certain jurisdictions.
